pattern: Bitmask DP over subsets of words: dp[mask][last] = minimum-length superstring that covers exactly the words in mask and ends with word[last]; reconstruct path for the optimal.
hints: Precompute pairwise overlaps: overlap[i][j] is the length of the longest suffix of words[i] that is also a prefix of words[j]. Maximizing total overlap minimizes total length.
Define dp[mask][i] = shortest superstring covering the subset mask that ends with words[i]. Transition: dp[mask|1<<j][j] = min(dp[mask][i] + len(words[j]) - overlap[i][j]).
To reconstruct the actual string, store the parent (previous word index) alongside the DP, then follow the path to concatenate non-overlapping suffixes in order.

edge_inputs.description: three words with no overlap — concatenate in any order
single word — answer is the word itself
words where overlap order matters — greedy may fail
two words with mutual overlaps, pick best direction

stress.description: 8 random words of length 3-6 with low overlap probability
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = min(12, max(1, size))
    seen = set()
    words = []
    while len(words) < n:
        ln = rng.randint(3, 6)
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:8]) for _ in range(ln))
        # ensure no word is substring of another
        if any(w in other or other in w for other in words):
            continue
        words.append(w)
    return [words]
